records.ety: [L. temperantia: cf. F. temprance. See Temper, v. t.]

defs: Habitual moderation in regard to the indulgence of the natural appetites and passions; restrained or moderate indulgence; moderation; as, temperance in eating and drinking; temperance in the indulgence of joy or mirth; specifically, moderation, and sometimes abstinence, in respect to using intoxicating liquors.
Moderation of passion; patience; calmness; sedateness.
quotes: He calmed his wrath with goodly temperance.

defs: State with regard to heat or cold; temperature.
